asset_name: UI atlas — beat marker and note glyph set
shared_properties: The glyph set uses the warm cream and gold UI palette that pairs with the lane panel.
All glyphs remain compact enough for even spacing across the 1280px rhythm bar.
Every glyph cell stays aligned to a shared baseline for deterministic placement.
differences: This atlas centralizes the moving marker and note symbols used during live rhythm input.
The filled note uses more gold area than the empty note so required beats stand out quickly.
A slim lane tick cell supports evenly spaced beat subdivisions without additional texture files.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas-driven UI entity set that reuses a compact glyph sheet for the rhythm bar.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load ui/art_000016.png during UI boot and use atlas regions for the triangle marker, filled note, empty note, and bar ticks.
runtime_notes: Bind the moving marker position to BPM-driven timers while the glyph art remains static inside the atlas.
Keep all glyph anchors on whole pixels so note spacing stays stable at 60 FPS.
Expose atlas indices in a resource so gameplay, replay, and tutorial systems use one shared glyph mapping.

context.usage: Glyph atlas for the moving beat marker, filled note, empty note, and lane ticks.

shape.silhouette: Compact UI glyph strip containing a triangle marker, filled note, empty note, and vertical tick.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 192x32px with 4 cells of 48x32px and 0px spacing.
The triangle marker remains legible at a glance and points upward with a centered tip.
Filled and empty note glyphs are visually distinct when viewed over the dark lane panel.
